title: narcissus patulus subsp. vergellensis
intro: Narcissus patulus subsp. vergellensis, also known as the mountain daffodil, is a type of flowering bulbous perennial plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been used to treat a range of health issues, such as respiratory problems and skin conditions. The health benefits of Narcissus patulus subsp. vergellensis include its ability to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, and poultices, and is said to have a range of applications in traditional medicine, although its use should be carefully researched and monitored.
